phrases.intent_en: Ask for the best streets to explore in the old town
phrases.text_local: Quelles sont les rues les plus agréables à explorer dans le Vieil Aix ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel sohn lay ROO lay ploos ah-gray-AH-bluh ah ex-ploh-RAY dahn luh VYEHY EKS
phrases.cultural_note: The most atmospheric streets are Rue Gaston de Saporta (leading to the cathedral), Rue de l'Opéra, and the little Passage Agard. Getting deliberately lost in the medieval lanes is one of the great pleasures of Aix — the old town is small enough that you can't go far astray.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for directions to the nearest fountain
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve la fontaine la plus proche ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV lah fon-TEN lah ploo PROSH
phrases.cultural_note: Aix has over 40 fountains scattered through the old town — you are rarely more than a minute's walk from one. Look for the mossy baroque fountains fed by natural thermal springs; they maintain a constant flow year-round.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the best time to wander the old town
phrases.text_local: Quelle est la meilleure heure pour se promener dans le Vieil Aix ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel eh lah meh-YUHR UHR poor suh proh-muh-NAY dahn luh VYEHY EKS
phrases.cultural_note: Early morning (before 9h) and evening (after 18h) are magical in Vieil Aix — the lanes are quiet, the golden stone glows, and the fountains can be heard clearly. Midday in summer can be very hot; the narrow streets provide shade but tourist numbers peak then.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about local food shops in the area
phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il de bonnes épiceries fines dans le Vieil Aix ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee ah-TEEL duh bohn ay-pees-REE FEEN dahn luh VYEHY EKS
phrases.cultural_note: Vieil Aix has excellent food shopping: Lilamand (candied fruits since 1866), Béchard patisserie (their Mont Sainte-Victoire chocolate cake is legendary), and various olive oil shops. The streets around Place des Prêcheurs have the best concentration.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if cars are permitted in the old town lanes
phrases.text_local: Est-ce que les voitures sont autorisées dans le Vieil Aix ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess kuh lay vwah-TOOR sohn oh-toh-ree-ZAY dahn luh VYEHY EKS
phrases.cultural_note: Most of Vieil Aix is pedestrianised. A few streets allow resident and delivery vehicles at restricted hours. The entire area is most enjoyable on foot; bicycles and scooters should also be walked, not ridden, in the narrowest lanes.
